title: Rich Velvety Ukrainian Cornmeal Soup
description: A crowd‑pleasing, creamy soup that showcases the hearty texture of cornmeal with a blend of fresh vegetables and aromatic herbs, all while staying paleo‑friendly and gluten‑free.
instructions: In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the diced onion, carrot, and celery, sautéing until softened, about 5–6 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and smoked paprika, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring to a gentle simmer. Let it cook for 10 minutes to meld the flavors.
Stir in the cornmeal gradually, whisking to prevent lumps. Simmer for 8 minutes, stirring frequently, until the soup thickens to velvety consistency.
Reduce the heat to low and stir in the almond milk and chopped dill, heating through but not boiling.
Season with salt and black pepper, tasting and adjusting as desired.
Remove from heat and let the soup rest for 2 minutes.
Ladle the soup into bowls, garnish with additional dill or a drizzle of olive oil if desired.
Serve hot with crusty gluten‑free bread or a side salad.
Optional: Finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice for brightness.
